title: Trump’s allies are already lining up to apply to his $1.8 billion fund
description: Jan. 6 rioters, George Santos, Mark McCloskey and Rod Blagojevich: A wide range of figures are eyeing the president’s new settlement fund.

title: Even as anxieties grow under Trump, these swing voters aren't ready to back Democrats
description: Swing voters in North Carolina say they are frustrated with President Trump and the state of the economy, but aren't ready to abandon him or his party as the midterms inch closer.

title: Spencer Pratt is 'winning the internet,' but can he become mayor of Los Angeles?
description: Pratt, a former reality TV star, is flooding social media with edgy humor, AI slop and combative rhetoric as a way of grabbing attention and winning the vote of the very online. It's a strategy some political experts see as the future of online campaigning.

title: Liberal U.S. mayors team up with European counterparts to fight authoritarianism
description: Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.

title: Trump demands Senate fire parliamentarian who ruled against ballroom funding plan
description: The demand comes as Senate Republican leaders wrestle with whether to include money for the controversial project in a bill that funds immigration agencies.

title: At Coast Guard commencement, Trump tells grads to lead ‘national resurgence’
description: The president linked the force’s mission to his own priorities, domestically and in Iran, in an address at the military academy in Connecticut.

title: The DOJ is trying to let Trump and his companies off the hook for past tax problems
description: President Trump's settlement over the leak of his tax returns frees him and his company from any past IRS problems. Critics say that gives him a sweeping free pass from audits and tax penalties.

title: Supreme Court voting rights ruling could play a big role at the local level
description: The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.

title: Former senator calls on fellow Republicans to talk with the president more
description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Lamar Alexander, former Republican senator from Tennessee and governor of that state, about how he thinks current senators should respond to President Trump.

title: What swing voters in North Carolina have to say about Trump, the midterms and AI
description: Swing voters from North Carolina weigh in on the economy, President Trump, midterm voting intentions and AI.

title: In Georgia, Senate GOP primary heads to runoff where electability is top issue
description: Georgia was among six states where voters participated in primaries Tuesday. Its Senate race is among a handful that could determine party control next year.

title: The Trump paradox: What’s good for him is weighing down his party
description: The president has created a party that is loyal to him, even when he takes executive actions that anger the broader public. That dynamic leaves him weaker with those outside his base.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: Barney Frank, influential congressman and gay rights hero, dies at 86
description: The Massachusetts Democrat led a sweeping overhaul of the financial industry and was one of the first openly gay members of Congress.

title: U.S. grand jury indicts Raúl Castro, former Cuban president
description: The 94-year-old former leader of Cuba faces several charges, including four counts of murder for an attack on a humanitarian group more than 30 years ago.

title: The Justice Department gives Trump an unprecedented settlement
description: D.O.J. gave Trump and his family immunity from tax audits and set up a $1.8 billion fund for victims of "government weaponization." Former government lawyer Andrew Weissmann explains the settlement.

title: Barney Frank, former congressman and gay-rights pioneer, dies at 86
description: The former congressman was politically engaged until the end. As GBH reports, Even in hospice, Frank was actively working to spread the message of his forthcoming book, "The Hard Path to Unity: Why We Must Reform the Left to Rescue Democracy."

title: Pentagon says U.S. will cut thousands of troops from Europe
description: Poland was set to receive forces whose deployment was abruptly halted last week.

title: Vance and Rubio step behind the lectern — and deeper into 2028 territory
description: Parallel briefings by the two men offer a view of their contrasting styles.

title: Trump endorses Paxton in Texas Senate primary over incumbent Cornyn
description: The president faced pressure from his base to endorse the state’s attorney general, Ken Paxton. Advisers had urged him to support Sen. John Cornyn, arguing that Paxton has too much baggage.

title: Admiral accuses lawmaker of ‘inappropriate’ remark at Iran war hearing
description: Adm. Brad Cooper, the head of U.S. Central Command, appeared frustrated with questions from House Democrats who pressed him on a range of issues about the conflict.

title: Trump’s extraordinary $1.8 billion legal fund to his allies
description: Why the enormous fund is so controversial.

title: Trump shows off White House ballroom construction as funding stalls in Congress
description: The president offered journalists the closest look yet at the construction project, where photographers captured images of the work in progress and Trump touted security measures.
